name: Billy Mitchell
abstract: William Melvin Mitchell (November 3, 1926 – April 18, 2001) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist.

interviews.title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

concerts.label: The Sensation of Music at Newport '61: Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Midnight Performance: The Sensation of Music at Newport '61/ Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: An Evening with Joe Williams and Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: And by the end of that set he turned around and he said, “From now on you’re my drummer.” And I did like twelve years of Wednesday nights at Sonny’s Jazz Club.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: And the tenor player is Billy Mitchell of the Count Basie band... So I finally get called up... I turned to Billy Mitchell and I said, “Mr. Mitchell, thank you very much for letting me sit in with you. It was an honor.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 29, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: It was a fantastic band and in fact, we had more Basie men in that band than was in the Frank Foster band. We had Al Grey, Snooky Young, and myself. Billy Mitchell, Eddie Jones, Marshall Royal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And the reeds was Marshall Royal, baritone was Charlie Fowlkes, Frank Wess, Frank Foster, and Billy Mitchell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with Billy Mitchell at the Bluebird Inn... I worked there for over three years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I came down here -- I think it was '61 -- with Al Gray and Billy Mitchell. They had a sextet.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
